gabby_xz_xiang.name: Gabby XZ Xiang
gabby_xz_xiang.summary:
Gabby XZ Xiang is a rising force in junior badminton, already collecting 3 tournament titles among 48 career entries across 18 tournaments. The trajectory is unmistakable — this is a player whose best chapters are still being written.

A true triple-threat, Gabby competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 1 singles title, 1 doubles title, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three disciplines reveals a player with both the individual brilliance to dominate rallies and the court sense to thrive with a partner.

Gabby has demonstrated remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 13 different players across the career. Key partnerships:
  • Erela Liu — 13 events, 1 title together in GD
  • Aarav Gupta — 3 events, 1 title together in XD
  • Adithya S Burisetty — 2 events in XD, best: #3

A veteran of the junior circuit, Gabby has competed across U13, U15 and U17, accumulating invaluable experience at every age group. That depth of competitive history is an asset that younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

With tournaments spanning Midwest, NE, South, Gabby isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 3 titles to show for it, Gabby is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to what dedication, talent, and love for the game can produce.

gabriel_sutanto.summary:

Competing in both singles and doubles, Gabrielle is developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as the competition intensifies at higher levels.

gagan_polineni.name: Gagan Polineni
gagan_polineni.summary:
Gagan Polineni broke through with a tournament title — a moment that separates contenders from champions. Across 55 entries in 24 tournaments, Gagan has shown the kind of competitive fire that produces breakthroughs, and there are surely more titles ahead.

A true triple-threat, Gagan competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #2, 1 doubles title. The ability to excel in all three disciplines reveals a player with both the individual brilliance to dominate rallies and the court sense to thrive with a partner.

Gagan has demonstrated remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 16 different players across the career. Key partnerships:
  • Ryan Shih — 9 events, 1 title together in BD
  • Amelia Wang — a trusted XD partner (4 events, best result: #5)

Having moved up to U17, Gagan is navigating the challenge of tougher competition at the higher age group. The adjustment period is natural — and with the pedigree Gagan brings, a return to the top is a matter of when, not if.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Gagan has competed across U13, U15 and U17, accumulating invaluable experience at every age group. That depth of competitive history is an asset that younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Gagan has competed across 5 different regions of the country (Midwest, NE, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel and test the game against diverse styles of play from coast to coast.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 1 title to show for it, Gagan is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to what dedication, talent, and love for the game can produce.

ganesh_karthik.name: Ganesh Karthik
ganesh_karthik.summary:
Ganesh Karthik is a tenacious competitor with 3 quarterfinal-or-better finishes across 10 entries in 5 tournaments. The foundation is solid, and the upward trajectory is clear — deeper runs are on the horizon.

Competing in both singles and doubles, Ganesh is developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as the competition intensifies at higher levels.

Ganesh has competed at both U13 and U15, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as the competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With tournaments spanning NE, NorCal, SoCal, Ganesh isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Ganesh's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

garret_tan.name: Garret Tan
garret_tan.summary:
Garret Tan is a U.S. Junior National Champion — a distinction that places Garret among the finest junior players in the country. With 5 career tournament victories across 14 tournaments and 36 total entries, this is a player who consistently rises to the biggest moments.

A true triple-threat, Garret competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 1 singles title, 2 doubles titles, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three disciplines reveals a player with both the individual brilliance to dominate rallies and the court sense to thrive with a partner.

Signature achievements: National Champion in BD U15 • National Finalist in BS U15 • 1 ORC title (across 1 tournament) • U.S. Selection Event top-1 finisher.

Garret has demonstrated remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 10 different players across the career. Key partnerships:
  • Tian Qi Alan Zhang — 6 events together in BD/U17, producing an outstanding 2 titles
  • Audrey Chang — 5 events, 1 title together in U17/XD

The 2024-2025 season has been a step up, with 2 titles across 3 tournaments — an improvement that shows Garret is still ascending. The best may yet be ahead.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Garret has competed across U15, U17 and U19, accumulating invaluable experience at every age group. That depth of competitive history is an asset that younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Garret has competed across 5 different regions of the country (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel and test the game against diverse styles of play from coast to coast.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 5 titles to show for it, Garret is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to what dedication, talent, and love for the game can produce.

grace_cheng.name: Grace Cheng
grace_cheng.summary:
Grace Cheng is a proven champion with 7 tournament titles to go along with 9 Finals and 13 semifinal appearances across 18 tournaments. That kind of consistency at the top doesn't happen by accident — it's the product of relentless preparation and fierce competitive instinct.

A true triple-threat, Grace competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #2, 3 doubles titles, 4 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three disciplines reveals a player with both the individual brilliance to dominate rallies and the court sense to thrive with a partner.

Signature achievements: National Finalist in GS U11, XD U11 • 5 ORC titles (across 5 tournaments) • U.S. Selection Event top-3 finisher.

Grace has demonstrated remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 9 different players across the career. Key partnerships:
  • Zelenia Shen — 11 events together in GD/U13, producing an outstanding 3 titles
  • Harrison Zhang — 6 events, 1 title together in U13/XD
  • Leo Ye — a trusted XD partner (5 events, best result: #2)

The 2025-2026 season has been a step up, with 3 titles across 4 tournaments — an improvement that shows Grace is still ascending. The best may yet be ahead.

Grace has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as the competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Grace has competed across 6 different regions of the country (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel and test the game against diverse styles of play from coast to coast.

With 13 seeded entries across the career, Grace consistently earns the respect of tournament committees — a testament to the results put up on the court.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 7 titles to show for it, Grace is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to what dedication, talent, and love for the game can produce.
